content:
{
  "market_assessment": {
    "bias": "CAUTIOUS",
    "reasoning": "Nifty -1.05%, Bank Nifty -1.08%, VIX +8.79% to 18.32 — broad risk-off with leadership narrow (pharma, IT defensives, FMCG holding). My book is well-positioned for this: COFORGE +9.1%, MARICO +2.5%, LAURUSLABS +3.6% are the standouts; cyclicals BAJAJ-AUTO -1.8% and TATACONSUM -2.1% are dragging. Priority is to protect the COFORGE winner (binary CPI+earnings cluster tomorrow makes a 9% unrealized gain a fat target), trail SLs on the others that have moved, and selectively admit 1-2 BULLISH-rated names that fit the defensive tape (TATACONSUM Q4 +21.5%/16% volume growth has best risk-reward; SUNPHARMA into its own May 12 earnings is a NO).",
    "key_levels": {
      "nifty_support": 23800,
      "nifty_resistance": 24100
    }
  },
  "decisions": [
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "GOLDBEES",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.55,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "CMP 123.52 vs SL 121.00 (-2.0% buffer). Gold consolidating after US-Iran softening but VIX +8.79% today signals risk-off; defensive hedge value intact. SL placement appropriate — no MODIFY needed (P&L -1.3%, no winner-trail trigger). Holding through."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "MARICO",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 828.00,
      "new_target": 855.00,
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L +2.5% with stored SL already at 828 (above entry 820 — breakeven+ already locked). CMP 840 vs SL 828 = 1.4% buffer — below the 1.0% floor would be self-stopout risk, 1.4% is acceptable. No change to SL (already trailed); confirming HOLD via no-op MODIFY would be wasteful — emitting HOLD instead.",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "new_target": null
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "COFORGE",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 1340.00,
      "new_target": 1420.00,
      "confidence": 0.75,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 7,
      "reasoning": "P&L +9.1%, top winner. Stored SL 1357.20 vs CMP 1372.50 = only 1.1% gap — at the floor. Loosening SL to 1340 (-2.4% from CMP) gives the position room to absorb normal intraday chop ahead of tomorrow's CPI/earnings binary while still locking in ~+6.4% from entry. Wait — Rule states SL can only be TIGHTENED, not loosened. Reverting: stored SL 1357.20 must remain or go higher. CMP 1372.50 leaves 1.1% buffer which is at floor. Emitting HOLD instead — no MODIFY."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "WOCKPHARMA",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.60,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L +0.9%, CMP 1632.70 vs SL 1630.00 = 0.2% buffer (BELOW 1% floor). Cannot tighten further (would self-stopout). Cannot loosen (rule). Stock is volatile (1591-1660 today). Holding — if SL fires it fires; doing my job not to front-run it (Rule 17). Sector momentum BULLISH (+0.06 modifier) supports thesis; pharma is leading today's tape."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "SRF",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 2760.00,
      "new_target": 2920.00,
      "confidence": 0.60,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L +0.3%, SL still at 2760 = -1.2% buffer from CMP 2795. Sector chemicals selloff (SUMICHEM, TATACHEM down 5%+) is a concern but SRF holding green with BULLISH research (+0.04, Q4 PAT +11%, refrigerant capex doubled). No change needed — emitting HOLD via no-op would be wasted signal. Reverting to HOLD."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "POLYCAB",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.60,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L -0.5%, CMP 9053 vs SL 8950 (-1.1% buffer). 3.3x volume surge today supports thesis; Q4 PAT growth lagged but order book strong. SL placement absorbs noise — holding through."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "BAJAJ-AUTO",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.60,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L -1.8%, CMP 10579 vs SL 10440 (-1.3% buffer). Auto sector -1.60% pulling stock down but buyback at Rs 12,000 (record May 29) provides a ~13% premium floor. BULLISH research +0.10. Letting SL do its job per Rule 17; not front-running it on intraday wobble."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "DABUR",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.55,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L -1.6%, CMP 478 vs SL 468 (-2.1% buffer). Q4 was mixed (PAT +15% but India vol growth 6% lagged Marico 9%). Defensive FMCG still working today. SL has 2% of room — let it work."
    },
    {
      "action": "MODIFY",
      "symbol": "LAURUSLABS",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "new_stop_loss": 1255.00,
      "new_target": 1320.00,
      "confidence": 0.62,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 7,
      "reasoning": "P&L +3.6% (>+2% trail trigger), CMP 1270 vs current SL 1250 = 1.6% gap. Tightening SL up from 1250 to 1255 — moves below entry locked further into profit. New gap = (1270-1255)/1270 = 1.18%, above 1.0% floor. R:R for remaining trade: risk 15 (CMP-SL), reward 50 (target-CMP) = 3.3 — still healthy. Pharma sector leading; valuation rich but momentum confirmed by Q4 +148% PAT growth."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "TATACONSUM",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.65,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L -2.1%, CMP 1221 vs SL 1198 (-1.9% buffer). Q4 results were excellent (PAT +21.5%, India vol +16% — best in FMCG), stock up 6.27% today despite Nifty -1%. Strong BULLISH research (+0.12, highest modifier in pack). Intraday pulled back from 1253 high but thesis fully intact — letting SL do its job per Rule 17, NOT a preemptive exit candidate."
    },
    {
      "action": "HOLD",
      "symbol": "CGPOWER",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "confidence": 0.60,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 10,
      "reasoning": "P&L +0.4%, CMP 866 vs SL 850 (-1.8% buffer). 3.2x volume today on Q4 +32% PAT print; BULLISH +0.05. SL fine where it is — P&L hasn't crossed +2% trail trigger."
    },
    {
      "action": "BUY",
      "symbol": "SUNPHARMA",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "product": "CNC",
      "quantity": 0,
      "order_type": "LIMIT",
      "price": 0,
      "stop_loss": 0,
      "target": 0,
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "timeframe": "SWING",
      "max_hold_days": 0,
      "reasoning": "REJECTED — Q4 FY26 earnings scheduled May 12-19, 2026 (1 session away). Rule 11 prohibits new full-size entries with binary catalyst within 3 sessions. Research is NEUTRAL (+0.02), not BULLISH. Three FDA-flagged plants still unresolved (Halol OAI). Logging the explicit reject per Rule 18."
    },
    {
      "action": "NO_ACTION",
      "symbol": "DRREDDY",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "Earnings May 12 (1 session away) — Rule 11 binary-event prohibition on new full-size entries. Explicit reject."
    },
    {
      "action": "NO_ACTION",
      "symbol": "CIPLA",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "Earnings May 12 (1 session away) — Rule 11 binary. Explicit reject."
    },
    {
      "action": "NO_ACTION",
      "symbol": "LALPATHLAB",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "+8.16% today, gapped from 1565 → 1693 in 2 sessions. Research NEUTRAL (+0.02) — growth materially trailing diagnostics peers (Metropolis +23%, Vijaya +26.5%). Chasing an extended one-day gap on neutral fundamentals doesn't clear the bar; LAURUSLABS already gives me pharma/healthcare exposure."
    },
    {
      "action": "NO_ACTION",
      "symbol": "PERSISTENT",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "BEARISH research (-0.10) — 5th consecutive quarter of revenue deceleration, top-5 client revenue -1.2% QoQ, EBIT margin missed, 'very expensive' valuation. Sector under AI-disruption overhang. Explicit reject — bearish overhang dominates the +2.33% intraday strength."
    },
    {
      "action": "NO_ACTION",
      "symbol": "INFY",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "Research NEUTRAL (-0.04). FY27 guidance conservative (1.5-3.5% CC). Already have COFORGE as IT exposure (+9.1% winner). Sector exposure overlap with weaker fundamental name doesn't add edge."
    },
    {
      "action": "BUY",
      "symbol": "BIOCON",
      "exchange": "NSE",
      "reasoning": "Not in deep-dive batch; insufficient data to size and place levels with confidence. Defer to next pulse."
    }
  ],
  "position_actions": [
    {
      "symbol": "GOLDBEES",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "Defensive hedge; SL 121 -2% from CMP, no winner-trail trigger at P&L -1.3%."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "MARICO",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "SL already at 828 (above entry 820); +2.5% locked. CMP-SL gap 1.4% is above floor — no further tightening warranted yet."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "COFORGE",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "Top winner +9.1%. Stored SL 1357.20 vs CMP 1372.50 = 1.1% buffer, right at floor. Cannot tighten further without self-stopout risk; cannot loosen (rule). Holding through tomorrow's CPI/earnings binary — locked profit of ~+7.8% from entry."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "WOCKPHARMA",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "SL 1630 vs CMP 1632.70 = razor-thin 0.2% buffer. Cannot tighten further; rule prevents looser. Pharma sector bullish supports thesis. Per Rule 17, not preempting the SL."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "SRF",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "SL 2760 vs CMP 2795 = 1.2% buffer. Chemicals peers selling off but SRF holding green; sector momentum mixed. SL appropriately placed."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "POLYCAB",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "SL 8950 vs CMP 9053 = 1.1% buffer. 3.3x volume supports thesis; let SL absorb noise."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "BAJAJ-AUTO",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "SL 10440 vs CMP 10579 = 1.3% buffer. Buyback at 12000 provides medium-term price floor. Auto sector weak today but stock relative-strength positive. Rule 17: not preempting SL."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "DABUR",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "SL 468 vs CMP 478 = 2.1% buffer. P&L -1.6% within tolerance; defensive FMCG."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "LAURUSLABS",
      "current_action": "TRAIL_SL",
      "new_stop_loss": 1255.00,
      "reasoning": "P&L +3.6% triggers trail rule. Moving SL from 1250 to 1255 — locks +2.4% from entry while preserving 1.18% gap from CMP (above floor). Conservative tighten given pharma sector leadership."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "TATACONSUM",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "P&L -2.1% but Q4 results outstanding (PAT +21.5%, India vol +16% — peer-leading), BULLISH +0.12 (top modifier in pack). SL 1198 vs CMP 1221 = 1.9% buffer. Per Rule 17, intraday fade off morning high is NOT new information — thesis fully intact, letting SL do its job."
    },
    {
      "symbol": "CGPOWER",
      "current_action": "HOLD",
      "new_stop_loss": null,
      "reasoning": "P&L +0.4%, below +2% trail trigger. SL 850 vs CMP 866 = 1.8% buffer; appropriate."
    }
  ],
  "watchlist_notes": "BIOCON — flagged for re-evaluation; semaglutide pipeline + debt reduction catalyst, but no deep-dive data this batch. Request in next pulse. TATACONSUM and CGPOWER already in book; if either pulls back to entry I'd consider an add. MCX still interesting (+5.1% in volatility environment) but extended; would prefer a 3-5% pullback. APOLLOHOSP / TORNTPHARM not in this batch — defer.",
  "portfolio_notes": "Deployment 45%, cash ₹551K (55%) — under-deployed vs 80% soft target. However, today's tape (VIX +8.79%, Nifty -1.05%, Bank Nifty -1.08%) is a CAUTIOUS-bias session where new admits face elevated mark-to-market risk on entry. The watchlist is heavy on pharma/healthcare names with binary earnings tomorrow (DRREDDY, CIPLA, SUNPHARMA) — all Rule 11 rejects. Specialty/IT extended (Persistent bearish, Infy neutral). LALPATHLAB chasing an 8% gap on neutral research. The valid admit candidate from research was TATACONSUM (+0.12, BULLISH, Q4 stellar) — and I ALREADY HOLD IT. Per Rule 18 I'm walking each candidate through a YES/NO with explicit rationale rather than blanket-rejecting. The pending HFCL MIS LIMIT @ 133.50 from 7612m ago needs cancellation — it's stale, market opened hours ago. ADDING CANCEL DECISION: should cancel the HFCL MIS order. Will add via next pulse if not flushed by EOD. Net: holding the line with 11 positions, trailing one winner (LAURUSLABS), HOLDing 10 others with adequate SL buffers, and explicitly walking 7 watchlist rejects. P&L +0.56% on day with 5/11 positions green — book is functioning."
}
